_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass (SWORD VARIANT) — TIER BREAK from Tier 2 (Footman) to Tier 3 (Sergeant). Take the L6 'Veteran Sergeant' composited keeper and produce the L7 'Footman Sergeant' variant.
_meta.level_beat_doc: UnitProgression/Infantry/Infantry_Progression_Plan.md (L7 — Footman Sergeant, TIER BREAK) (SWORD VARIANT — see Infantry_Sword_Progression.md)
history: v1 (2026-05-07): generated by build_sword_prompts.py from `infantry_edit_L6_to_L7.json` (mace variant). Bulk language substitutions: mace→sword, wooden haft→leather grip, iron spikes→cross-guard, mace head→sword blade, etc. Sword-specific weapon beats applied at L3, L4, L5, L6, L7, L10 (see WEAPON_BEAT_OVERRIDES in build_sword_prompts.py for the per-level deltas).
_meta.tier: Tier 3 — Footman Sergeant (TIER BREAK from Tier 2) — SWORD VARIANT
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chained (Rule 2 — composite-only pipeline locked for Infantry): two inputs = L6 composite + L1_Sword_Base scale anchor. Tier-break composite step uses --dilate 20.

prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The first input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the L6 'Veteran Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the L1 base. You MUST preserve every L2 + L3 + L4 + L5 + L6 feature shown in the first input image: (a) the FULL STEEL BREASTPLATE covering the entire torso from collarbone (where it joins the bevor) down to the leather belt at the waist; the breastplate has a slight medial ridge (the new L7 step ADDS articulated horizontal lames across the abdomen — the breastplate is not removed); (b) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle (visible between the tassets at the front); (c) the STEEL TASSETS hanging from the belt at both hips with slight outward flare and visible dark-iron rivets at the top edge — the LIVERY tunic skirt remains visible as a narrow red+mustard band through the front gap between the two tassets; (d) the LAYERED 2-DISC STEEL PAULDRONS on both shoulders (the new L7 step KEEPS the 2-disc layering — 3-lame pauldrons arrive at L8); (e) the STEEL VAMBRACES on both forearms (the new L7 gauntlets EXTEND from these vambraces continuously — vambraces are not removed); (f) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap and brass stud on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (visible at upper edge of right tasset); (g) the BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with cream bone-handle knife on the anatomical-LEFT hip (visible at upper edge of left tasset); (h) the BRASS QUILLON CAPS at the cross-guard tips (small warm-brass bulb caps at each end of the cross-guard, distinctly warm brass tone against the cool steel cross-guard) — these MUST REMAIN at this level (the new L7 sword blade replaces the wooden head only; the sword grip and grip cord remain unchanged); (i) the WARM-BROWN SWORD LEATHER GRIP with the two L6 steel grip-rings running along its length and three rivets — sword grip and strip remain unchanged at L7 (only the sword HEAD changes from arming sword to longsword); (j) the STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with central steel boss, four iron cross strap-bands, continuous iron rim band, and L2 rim rivets (the new L7 step EXPANDS the shield by ~10% and adds an embossed sun-burst boss pattern, but the round shape and the existing features are preserved); (k) the STEEL SABATONS over the warm-brown leather boots — toe caps and ankle straps remain. DO NOT revert any of these features. The second input image is provided ONLY as a scale and framing reference — match its character-to-canvas proportions exactly; do NOT apply any of its content. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the first input image, marking the TIER BREAK from Tier 2 Footman to Tier 3 Sergeant. The unit transitions from leather-trim plate to articulated full plate; the sword blade transitions from wooden to flanged steel; the hands become fully metalled; the head gains a steel sallet helm worn over the mail coif. Silhouette mightiness rule: the helm adds vertical extent at the head and the shield grows in diameter. (1) OPEN-FACE STEEL SALLET HELM WORN OVER THE MAIL COIF — add a cool silver-gray polished steel domed helm covering the top and sides of the head, with a forward-projecting brim above the eyes. The sallet is rounded at the dome, has a subtle crest ridge running front-to-back along the top, and projects forward by approximately 1.5 inches over the brow as a flat brim that casts a soft shadow over the upper face. The helm has visible dark-iron rivets along the brim seam (~6 rivets evenly spaced across the front brim). The helm DOES NOT have a visor, half-visor, or any face cover — the FACE remains FULLY VISIBLE below the brim: the eyes, nose, mustache, and beard are all clearly visible under the brim's shadow. The mail coif's BACK-OF-NECK and SHOULDER drape remains visible behind and below the helm — the helm sits OVER the coif and the coif drape continues from the lower edge of the helm down to the breastplate's collar line at the back and sides. The brim shape is NOT a wide kettle-helm brim — it is a forward-projecting sallet brim, broader at the front than at the sides. (2) REINFORCED CUIRASS WITH 3 ARTICULATED HORIZONTAL LAMES — modify the existing full breastplate to show three articulated horizontal lames (steel bands) across the lower half (abdomen) of the cuirass, between roughly the bottom of the sternum and the belt. The three lames are evenly spaced horizontal bands of cool silver-gray steel, each approximately 1 inch tall, with thin shadow lines between them suggesting articulation joints. The medial ridge running vertically down the breastplate is now thicker and more pronounced (a raised central ridge) and continues across all three lames. The upper half of the cuirass (collar to bottom of sternum) remains a single smooth piece. The lames give the abdomen a visibly more articulated look — the cuirass is no longer a single flat sheet but reads as a layered cuirass+lames assembly. (3) STEEL GAUNTLETS REPLACING THE BARE HANDS — add articulated steel finger-plates and a steel knuckle-plate over the previously bare hands. The vambraces on both forearms now EXTEND continuously into gauntlets at the wrist (no break in the metal between forearm and hand). Each gauntlet has: (i) a steel knuckle plate covering the back of the hand and knuckles, with four small dark-iron rivets at the knuckle line; (ii) articulated steel finger plates covering each of the four fingers and the thumb, with two small steel segments per finger to suggest articulated finger joints. The gauntlets are cool silver-gray polished steel matching the vambraces. The fingers of the anatomical-RIGHT gauntlet wrap around the upper sword grip of the sword just below the new longsword steel blade; the anatomical-LEFT gauntlet remains hidden behind the round shield. The bare warm peachy-tan skin of the hands is FULLY GONE — no skin visible on either hand. (4) REPLACE THE ARMING SWORD WITH A LONGSWORD (HAND-AND-A-HALF) — REPLACE the L1 arming sword with a LONGER LONGSWORD. The new longsword has: (i) a longer steel blade — approximately 36-38 inches long (up from the arming sword's ~30 inches), with the same cool silver-gray polished steel and a deeper central fuller running the length of the blade; (ii) a wider, more pronounced cross-guard — approximately 6 inches wide tip-to-tip with the L4 downward-curled quillon tips preserved and the L3 brass quillon caps still at the tips, but the cross-guard body is now thicker and more substantial; (iii) a longer leather-wrapped grip — approximately 6 inches long (up from ~4 inches), allowing hand-and-a-half use, still warm-brown leather wrapped diagonally with the L6 steel grip-rings at top and bottom; (iv) a HEAVIER WHEEL POMMEL — the L5 wheel pommel remains but is now slightly larger (~2.5 inches diameter, up from 2 inches) to balance the longer blade. The L5 etched maker's mark on the ricasso is preserved. The longsword is approximately 10-15% LARGER overall in silhouette than the arming sword (silhouette mightiness rule — the blade reads visibly longer at thumbnail).  (5) LARGER ROUND SHIELD WITH EMBOSSED SUN-BURST BOSS — expand the existing steel-faced round shield by approximately 10% in diameter (silhouette mightiness rule — visibly larger than at L6). The central steel boss is now embossed with a SUN-BURST PATTERN: a raised central dome with 8 short triangular rays radiating outward from the dome, each ray approximately 1.5 inches long, all rendered in cool silver-gray steel as raised relief detail. The continuous iron rim band gains additional dark-iron studs evenly spaced around its circumference (now ~16 visible studs total, up from 8 at L6). The four iron cross strap-bands and the existing rim rivets remain. The shield's flat face is still cool silver-gray polished steel; the new sun-burst boss is the dominant ornament at the center. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE FIRST INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the first input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with single bright catchlight; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin with rosy blush; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks (now framed at the top by the new helm brim, at the bottom by the bevor / upper-chest steel) — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild; his dark mustache; the mail coif's back-of-neck and shoulder drape (the new helm sits OVER the coif at the top of the head); the LIVERY skirt band visible through the front gap between the two tassets; the wide leather belt with iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip; the brown leather knife sheath with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; the layered 2-disc steel pauldrons on both shoulders; the steel vambraces on both forearms (extending continuously into the new gauntlets); the leather grip wrap on the sword; the two steel grip-rings on the sword grip; the sword leather grip (head replaced per Edit Goal item 4; sword grip remains wooden); the steel-faced round shield (now larger and with embossed boss per Edit Goal item 5); the steel sabatons over warm-brown leather boots (toe caps and ankle straps); the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers visible at the calf below the breastplate-tassets line; the warm-brown leather boots with darker folded ankle cuff visible at calf, heel, and central foot. ===== POSE (must match the first input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE SWORD ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent ~120 degrees, gauntleted hand grips the sword's leather grip just below the new longsword blade, sword blade dangling at hip height angled down. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, forearm angled across the body, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height with face presenting toward viewer at slight three-quarter angle, gauntleted hand hidden behind the shield. Calm guard stance — both arms static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin (face only — hands now metal), saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match the SECOND input image exactly — L1_Base scale anchor) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall), approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide). TOP of helm dome at approximately 16.6% from top of canvas (the new helm replaces the previous mail-coif top in the silhouette envelope but the silhouette top is at the same vertical position). BOTTOM of boots at ~16.6% from bottom of canvas. Character vertically centered. ~18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, ~20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT cover the face — the L7 sallet helm is OPEN-FACE only; eyes, nose, mustache, and beard remain fully visible under the brim's shadow; DO NOT add a half-visor, closed visor, eye-slit visor, or any face cover (half-visor arrives at L8, closed visor at L9); DO NOT add horns, crests, plumes, or any vertical helm extension other than the subtle dome ridge (horns arrive at L9, central crest at L10); DO NOT remove the mail coif's back-of-neck drape — the coif drape stays visible behind and below the new helm; DO NOT cover the entire head with mail — the new helm sits over the coif at the top, the coif drape remains at the back of the neck and shoulders; DO NOT make the helm a wide kettle-helm with a brim wider than the dome — it is a sallet (forward-projecting brim, dome wider than the brim sides); DO NOT make the gauntlets and the vambraces separate pieces with a visible seam between them — they are continuous metal from forearm to fingertip; DO NOT leave any bare skin on the hands — both hands FULLY metalled with articulated finger-plates and knuckle-plates; DO NOT revert the breastplate to a single flat sheet — the cuirass MUST show three articulated horizontal lames across the abdomen at L7; DO NOT add scale-mail across the cuirass — the cuirass surface is smooth steel plate (lames are subtle articulation joints, not scales); DO NOT extend the lames upward into the upper chest — the upper half of the cuirass remains smooth single-piece; DO NOT extend tassets longer than at L6 — tasset extension to mid-thigh arrives at L8; DO NOT add lames / vertical articulation to the tassets at L7 — they remain single flat plates at L7 (3-lame articulated tassets arrive at L8); DO NOT add a third or fourth disc to the pauldrons — they remain layered 2-disc at L7; DO NOT add gold rivets, gold trim, gold band, gold buckle, gold accents, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at L8; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, or filigree; DO NOT make the longsword blade a tapered shape — the new sword blade is a CYLINDRICAL/STAR-SHAPED solid steel head with 6 vertical flanges, NOT tapered; DO NOT keep the deepened central fuller or the blade shape on the sword blade — those are FULLY REPLACED by the flanged steel head; DO NOT add spikes to the new longsword blade — flanges replace spikes; the flanges have sharp edges but are not pointed spikes; DO NOT make the longsword blade smaller than the previous wooden head — it is ~10-15% LARGER (silhouette mightiness rule); DO NOT remove the leather grip wrap on the sword; DO NOT remove the two steel grip-rings on the sword grip; DO NOT replace the sword leather grip with metal — the sword grip remains warm-brown wood at L7; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT shrink the shield diameter — it grows ~10% larger at L7; DO NOT change the boss color to gold — the boss remains cool silver-gray steel (gold boss embellishment arrives at L9.5); DO NOT add a cross emblem on the shield face — that arrives at L9.5; DO NOT remove the iron rim band, rim rivets, or cross strap-bands; DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the sword; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
